title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: CDC AtlasPlus reports many outcomes county level. analyses, especially geographic access analyses, may want estimates smaller geographic unit Census tract. CDCAtlas includes helper functions extrapolating county-level AtlasPlus counts Census tracts using tract-level Census denominators. basic idea : Retrieve county-level CDC AtlasPlus counts. Retrieve tract-level Census population denominators. Match tract parent county. Allocate county’s reported cases across tracts proportion tract’s share relevant county population. example, tract contains 2% county’s population relevant denominator, receives 2% county’s reported cases. population-weighted extrapolation, direct CDC tract-level surveillance estimate.

title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: Tract-level extrapolation requires tract-level population denominators U.S. Census Bureau. CDCAtlas uses tidycensus package retrieve denominators. use CDCAtlas retrieve AtlasPlus county, state, national data, need set tidycensus. However, use extrapolate_to_tract = TRUE, need: tidycensus package installed Census API key API key saved R environment can install tidycensus CRAN: request free Census API key U.S. Census Bureau install using tidycensus::census_api_key(). See https://walker-data.com/tidycensus/ details.
code: install.packages("tidycensus")

title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: method assumes , within county stratum, cases distributed across tracts proportion relevant Census denominator. example: unstratified HIV prevalence may allocated using total tract population sex-stratified counts may allocated using tract population sex age-stratified counts may allocated using tract population age race/ethnicity-stratified counts may allocated using tract population race/ethnicity can useful geographic access models, interpreted observed tract-level surveillance data.

title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: simplest use case extrapolating county-level data tracts using total tract population. returned data contain one row per tract, county-level AtlasPlus count allocated tracts. typical output includes columns : expect columns identifying: tract parent county AtlasPlus disease year county-level cases tract denominator county denominator tract share county denominator extrapolated tract cases key calculation : , generally:
code: hiv_tract <- get_atlas(   disease = "hiv",   year = 2022,   geography = "county",   extrapolate_to_tract = TRUE ) head(hiv_tract) #> # A tibble: 6 × 26 #>    year tract_fips  tract_name            county_fips race_ethnicity sex   age   #>   <dbl> <chr>       <chr>                 <chr>       <chr>          <chr> <chr> #> 1  2022 01001020100 Census Tract 201; Au… 01001       All races/eth… Both… Ages… #> 2  2022 01001020100 Census Tract 201; Au… 01001       All races/eth… Both… Ages… #> 3  2022 01001020200 Census Tract 202; Au… 01001       All races/eth… Both… Ages… #> 4  2022 01001020200 Census Tract 202; Au… 01001       All races/eth… Both… Ages… #> 5  2022 01001020300 Census Tract 203; Au… 01001       All races/eth… Both… Ages… #> 6  2022 01001020300 Census Tract 203; Au… 01001       All races/eth… Both… Ages… #> # ℹ 19 more variables: tract_population_acs <dbl>, county_population_acs <dbl>, #> #   indicator <fct>, county_name <fct>, data_status <fct>, transmission <fct>, #> #   rate100000 <dbl>, county_population_atlas <dbl>, lowerci_rate <dbl>, #> #   upperci_rate <dbl>, rse <dbl>, lowerci_cases <dbl>, upperci_cases <dbl>, #> #   state_fips <chr>, state_name <fct>, state_cases <dbl>, county_cases <dbl>, #> #   tract_cases <dbl>, tract_noncases <dbl> names(hiv_tract) #>  [1] "year"                    "tract_fips"              #>  [3] "tract_name"              "county_fips"             #>  [5] "race_ethnicity"          "sex"                     #>  [7] "age"                     "tract_population_acs"    #>  [9] "county_population_acs"   "indicator"               #> [11] "county_name"             "data_status"             #> [13] "transmission"            "rate100000"              #> [15] "county_population_atlas" "lowerci_rate"            #> [17] "upperci_rate"            "rse"                     #> [19] "lowerci_cases"           "upperci_cases"           #> [21] "state_fips"              "state_name"              #> [23] "state_cases"             "county_cases"            #> [25] "tract_cases"             "tract_noncases" tract_cases = county_cases * tract_population / county_population tract_cases = county_cases * tract_denominator / county_denominator

title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: extrapolation, tract-level estimates sum back original county-level counts, allowing small floating-point differences. clean allocation, difference column close zero.
code: hiv_tract %>%   group_by(county_fips, indicator) %>%   summarize(     county_cases = first(county_cases),     tract_cases_sum = sum(tract_cases, na.rm = TRUE),     difference = tract_cases_sum - county_cases,     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   arrange(desc(abs(difference))) %>%   head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   county_fips indicator      county_cases tract_cases_sum difference #>   <chr>       <fct>                 <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 51678       HIV prevalence        559.            559.    1.14e-13 #> 2 51530       HIV prevalence        462.            462.   -5.68e-14 #> 3 17187       HIV prevalence        126.            126.   -1.42e-14 #> 4 22107       HIV prevalence        113.            113.    1.42e-14 #> 5 16021       HIV prevalence         44.4            44.4   7.11e-15 #> 6 17047       HIV prevalence         46.7            46.7   7.11e-15

title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: sex-stratified AtlasPlus data, allocation denominator also sex-specific. means male county cases allocated across tracts using male population tract, female county cases allocated using female population tract. Conceptually: \[ \widehat{\text{tract cases}}_{\text{male}} = \text{county cases}_{\text{male}} \times \frac{\text{tract population}_{\text{male}}} {\text{county population}_{\text{male}}} \] \[ \widehat{\text{tract cases}}_{\text{female}} = \text{county cases}_{\text{female}} \times \frac{\text{tract population}_{\text{female}}} {\text{county population}_{\text{female}}} \] resulting data include one row per tract per sex stratum. can verify allocation within county sex stratum.
code: hiv_tract_sex <- get_atlas(   disease = "hiv",   year = 2022,   geography = "county",   stratify_by = "sex",   extrapolate_to_tract = TRUE ) hiv_tract_sex %>%   count(sex, indicator) #> # A tibble: 4 × 3 #>   sex    indicator          n #>   <chr>  <fct>          <int> #> 1 Female HIV diagnoses  85396 #> 2 Female HIV prevalence 85396 #> 3 Male   HIV diagnoses  85396 #> 4 Male   HIV prevalence 85396 hiv_tract_sex %>%   group_by(county_fips, sex, indicator) %>%   summarize(     county_cases = first(county_cases),     tract_cases_sum = sum(tract_cases, na.rm = TRUE),     difference = tract_cases_sum - county_cases,     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   arrange(desc(abs(difference))) %>%   head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   county_fips sex    indicator      county_cases tract_cases_sum difference #>   <chr>       <chr>  <fct>                 <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 37199       Male   HIV prevalence         95.2            95.2   1.42e-14 #> 2 51007       Male   HIV prevalence         50.7            50.7   7.11e-15 #> 3 51735       Male   HIV prevalence         48.0            48.0   7.11e-15 #> 4 72049       Male   HIV prevalence         63.0            63.0   7.11e-15 #> 5 13043       Female HIV prevalence         27.1            27.1  -3.55e-15 #> 6 32021       Male   HIV prevalence         22.8            22.8   3.55e-15

title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: Age-stratified extrapolation works way, except Census denominator based tract population within corresponding AtlasPlus age group. output contains one row per tract per age stratum. , diagnostic check whether tract estimates sum back original county count within county-age stratum.
code: hiv_tract_age <- get_atlas(   disease = "hiv",   year = 2022,   geography = "county",   stratify_by = "age",   extrapolate_to_tract = TRUE ) hiv_tract_age %>%   count(age, indicator) #> # A tibble: 12 × 3 #>    age   indicator          n #>    <chr> <fct>          <int> #>  1 13-24 HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  2 13-24 HIV prevalence 85396 #>  3 25-34 HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  4 25-34 HIV prevalence 85396 #>  5 35-44 HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  6 35-44 HIV prevalence 85396 #>  7 45-54 HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  8 45-54 HIV prevalence 85396 #>  9 55-64 HIV diagnoses  85396 #> 10 55-64 HIV prevalence 85396 #> 11 65+   HIV diagnoses  85396 #> 12 65+   HIV prevalence 85396 hiv_tract_age %>%   group_by(county_fips, age, indicator) %>%   summarize(     county_cases = first(county_cases),     tract_cases_sum = sum(tract_cases, na.rm = TRUE),     difference = tract_cases_sum - county_cases,     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   arrange(desc(abs(difference))) %>%   head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   county_fips age   indicator      county_cases tract_cases_sum difference #>   <chr>       <chr> <fct>                 <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 48301       35-44 HIV prevalence     0.0352                 0  -0.0352   #> 2 48301       45-54 HIV prevalence     0.0277                 0  -0.0277   #> 3 48301       35-44 HIV diagnoses      0.00149                0  -0.00149  #> 4 48301       45-54 HIV diagnoses      0.000725               0  -0.000725 #> 5 15005       13-24 HIV prevalence     0.000705               0  -0.000705 #> 6 15005       13-24 HIV diagnoses      0.000162               0  -0.000162

title: Extrapolation to tracts
text: CDC AtlasPlus commonly uses combined race/ethnicity variable. example, Hispanic/Latino represented single ethnicity category, remaining groups usually non-Hispanic race categories. Race/ethnicity-stratified extrapolation requires care Census AtlasPlus race/ethnicity categories may always align perfectly. package attempts use Census variables correspond AtlasPlus combined race/ethnicity categories, users inspect denominator mapping interpreting results.
code: hiv_tract_race <- get_atlas(   disease = "hiv",   year = 2022,   geography = "county",   stratify_by = "race",   extrapolate_to_tract = TRUE ) hiv_tract_race %>%   count(race_ethnicity, indicator) #> # A tibble: 14 × 3 #>    race_ethnicity                         indicator          n #>    <chr>                                  <fct>          <int> #>  1 American Indian/Alaska Native          HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  2 American Indian/Alaska Native          HIV prevalence 85396 #>  3 Asian                                  HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  4 Asian                                  HIV prevalence 85396 #>  5 Black/African American                 HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  6 Black/African American                 HIV prevalence 85396 #>  7 Hispanic/Latino                        HIV diagnoses  85396 #>  8 Hispanic/Latino                        HIV prevalence 85396 #>  9 Multiracial                            HIV diagnoses  85396 #> 10 Multiracial                            HIV prevalence 85396 #> 11 Native Hawaiian/Other Pacific Islander HIV diagnoses  85396 #> 12 Native Hawaiian/Other Pacific Islander HIV prevalence 85396 #> 13 White                                  HIV diagnoses  85396 #> 14 White                                  HIV prevalence 85396

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("VagishHemmige/CDCAtlas")

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
code: library(CDCAtlas) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: simple query retrieves one disease, one geography level, one year. returned object data frame can used directly tidyverse tools.
code: chlamydia_state <- get_atlas(   disease   = "chlamydia",   geography = "state",   year      = 2022 )  head(chlamydia_state) #>   indicator year  geography    data_status        race_ethnicity        sex #> 1 Chlamydia 2022    Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 2 Chlamydia 2022     Alaska Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 3 Chlamydia 2022    Arizona Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 4 Chlamydia 2022   Arkansas Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 5 Chlamydia 2022 California Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 6 Chlamydia 2022   Colorado Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #>              age                transmission rate100000  cases population #> 1 All age groups All transmission categories      612.1  31060    5074296 #> 2 All age groups All transmission categories      727.7   5338     733583 #> 3 All age groups All transmission categories      554.4  40796    7359197 #> 4 All age groups All transmission categories      588.3  17918    3045637 #> 5 All age groups All transmission categories      493.6 192647   39029342 #> 6 All age groups All transmission categories "Chlamydia", "Chlamydia", "Chlamydia", "Ch… #> $ year           <dbl> 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2022, 2… #> $ geography      <fct> "Alabama", "Alaska", "Arizona", "Arkansas", "California… #> $ data_status    <fct> "Not Suppressed", "Not Suppressed", "Not Suppressed", "… #> $ race_ethnicity <fct> "All races/ethnicities", "All races/ethnicities", "All … #> $ sex title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
code: chlamydia_state %>%   filter(!is.na(rate100000)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = reorder(geography, rate100000), y = rate100000)) +   geom_col() +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Chlamydia rate by state, 2022",     x = NULL,     y = "Rate per 100,000"   )

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: AtlasPlus can also return county-level data supported diseases. common workflow identify counties highest reported rates.
code: chlamydia_county <- get_atlas(   disease   = "chlamydia",   geography = "county",   year      = 2022 )  head(chlamydia_county) #>   indicator year            geography    data_status        race_ethnicity #> 1 Chlamydia 2022 Abbeville County, SC Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities #> 2 Chlamydia 2022       Ada County, ID Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities #> 3 Chlamydia 2022     Adair County, IA Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities #> 4 Chlamydia 2022    Acadia Parish, LA Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities #> 5 Chlamydia 2022  Accomack County, VA Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities #> 6 Chlamydia 2022     Adams County, CO Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities #>          sex            age                transmission rate100000 cases #> 1 Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories      496.8   121 #> 2 Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories      403.3  2093 #> 3 Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories      186.8    14 #> 4 Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories      676.7   384 #> 5 Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories      524.2   174 #> 6 Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories      591.8  3122 #>   population lowerci_rate upperci_rate rse lowerci_cases upperci_cases  fips #> 1      24356           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA 45001 #> 2     518907           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA 16001 #> 3       7494           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA 19001 #> 4      56744           NA chlamydia_county %>%   filter(!is.na(rate100000)) %>%   arrange(desc(rate100000)) %>%   select(geography, cases, rate100000) %>%   head(10) #>                       geography cases rate100000 #> 1 title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: Many AtlasPlus endpoints support stratification. example, can retrieve state-level gonorrhea data sex. Now can compare rates sex within state.
code: gonorrhea_sex <- get_atlas(   disease   = "gonorrhea",   geography = "state",   year      = 2022,   stratify_by = "sex" )  head(gonorrhea_sex) #>   indicator year geography    data_status        race_ethnicity    sex #> 1 Gonorrhea 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities   Male #> 2 Gonorrhea 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Female #> 3 Gonorrhea 2022    Alaska Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities   Male #> 4 Gonorrhea 2022    Alaska Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Female #> 5 Gonorrhea 2022   Arizona Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities   Male #> 6 Gonorrhea 2022   Arizona Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Female #>              age                transmission rate100000 cases population #> 1 All age groups All transmission categories      279.7  6901    2467360 #> 2 All age groups All transmission categories      238.3  6213    2606936 #> 3 All age groups All transmission categories      296.7  1145     385947 #> 4 All age groups All transmission categories      333.4  1159     347636 #> 5 All age groups All transmission categories      270.1  9936    3679034 #> 6 All age groups All transmission categories      177.2  6522    3680163 #>   lowerci_rate upperci_rate rse lowerci_cases upperci_cases fips #> 1           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 2           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 3           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA   02 #> 4           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA   02 #> 5           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA   04 #> 6           NA           NA  NA            NA            NA   04 gonorrhea_sex %>%   filter(!is.na(rate100000)) %>%   group_by(sex) %>%   slice_max(rate100000, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%   ungroup() %>%   ggplot(aes(x = reorder(geography, rate100000), y = rate100000)) +   geom_col() +   coord_flip() +   facet_wrap(~ sex, scales = "free_y") +   labs(     title = "States with highest gonorrhea rates by sex, 2022",     x = NULL,     y = "Rate per 100,000"   )

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: AtlasPlus also supports age-stratified queries many disease/geography combinations. can summarize national patterns aggregating across states.
code: hiv_age <- get_atlas(   disease   = "hiv",   geography = "state",   year      = 2022,   stratify_by = "age"  )  head(hiv_age) #>       indicator year geography    data_status        race_ethnicity        sex #> 1 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 2 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 3 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 4 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 5 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #> 6 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Both sexes #>     age                transmission rate100000 cases population lowerci_rate #> 1 13-24 All transmission categories       19.9   162     812982           NA #> 2 25-34 All transmission categories       34.5   227     657946           NA #> 3 35-44 All transmission categories       24.0   150     625076           NA #> 4 45-54 All transmission categories       13.6    84     618962           NA #> 5 55-64 All transmission categories        8.2    54     660461           NA #> 6   65+ All transmission categories        1.3    12     905888           NA #>   upperci_rate rse lowerci_cases upperci_cases fips #> 1           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 2           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 3           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 4           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 5           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 6           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 hiv_age_summary <- hiv_age %>%   group_by(age, indicator) %>%   summarize(     cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  hiv_age_summary #> # A tibble: 12 × 3 #>    age   indicator       cases #>    <fct> <fct>           <dbl> #>  1 13-24 HIV diagnoses    7142 #>  2 13-24 HIV prevalence  28242 #>  3 25-34 HIV diagnoses   14195 #>  4 25-34 HIV prevalence 166643 #>  5 35-44 HIV diagnoses    8320 #>  6 35-44 HIV prevalence 212167 #>  7 45-54 HIV diagnoses    4601 #>  8 45-54 HIV prevalence 238245 #>  9 55-64 HIV diagnoses    2873 #> 10 55-64 HIV prevalence 296458 #> 11 65+   HIV diagnoses     887 #> 12 65+   HIV prevalence 162449 hiv_age_summary%>%   filter(indicator=="HIV diagnoses")%>%   ggplot(mapping=aes(x = age, y = cases)) +   geom_col() +   labs(     title = "HIV diagnoses by age group, 2022",     x = "Age group",     y = "Cases"   )

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas

group_by(race_ethnicity, indicator) %>%   summarize(     cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   ) %>%   arrange(desc(cases))  hiv_race_summary #> # A tibble: 14 × 3 #>    race_ethnicity                         indicator       cases #>    <fct>                                  <fct>           <dbl> #>  1 Black/African American                 HIV prevalence 429419 #>  2 White                                  HIV prevalence 303461 #>  3 Hispanic/Latino                        HIV prevalence 287681 #>  4 Multiracial                            HIV prevalence  61928 #>  5 Native Hawaiian/Other Pacific Islander HIV diagnoses      78 hiv_race_summary%>%   filter(indicator=="HIV diagnoses")%>%   ggplot(mapping =aes(x = reorder(race_ethnicity, cases), y = cases)) +   geom_col() +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "HIV cases by race/ethnicity, 2022",     x = NULL,     y = "Cases"   )

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: can request combinations stratification variables supported underlying AtlasPlus endpoint. example, HIV data can queried sex age. allows direct comparison age distributions sex.
code: hiv_age_sex <- get_atlas(   disease   = "hiv",   geography = "state",   year      = 2022,   stratify_by = c("sex", "age")  )  head(hiv_age_sex) #>       indicator year geography    data_status        race_ethnicity  sex   age #> 1 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Male 13-24 #> 2 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Male 25-34 #> 3 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Male 35-44 #> 4 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Male 45-54 #> 5 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Male 55-64 #> 6 HIV diagnoses 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed All races/ethnicities Male   65+ #>                  transmission rate100000 cases population lowerci_rate #> 1 All transmission categories       34.9   143     410156           NA #> 2 All transmission categories       57.8   188     325041           NA #> 3 All transmission categories       33.6   102     303880           NA #> 4 All transmission categories       19.2    58     301809           NA #> 5 All transmission categories       11.3    36     318548           NA #> 6 All transmission categories        1.3     5     399964           NA #>   upperci_rate rse lowerci_cases upperci_cases fips #> 1           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 2           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 3           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 4           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 5           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 #> 6           NA  NA            NA            NA   01 hiv_age_sex_summary <- hiv_age_sex %>%   group_by(sex, age, indicator) %>%   summarize(     cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  hiv_age_sex_summary #> # A tibble: 24 × 4 #>    sex   age   indicator       cases #>    <fct> <fct> <fct>           <dbl> #>  1 Male  13-24 HIV diagnoses    6273 #>  2 Male  13-24 HIV prevalence  22841 #>  3 Male  25-34 HIV diagnoses   12129 #>  4 Male  25-34 HIV prevalence 141323 #>  5 Male  35-44 HIV diagnoses    6524 #>  6 Male  35-44 HIV prevalence 164560 #>  7 Male  45-54 HIV diagnoses    3344 #>  8 Male  45-54 HIV prevalence 172264 #>  9 Male  55-64 HIV diagnoses    2089 #> 10 Male  55-64 HIV prevalence 226435 #> # ℹ 14 more rows hiv_age_sex_summary%>%   filter(indicator=="HIV diagnoses")%>%   ggplot(aes(x = age, y = cases)) +   geom_col() +   facet_wrap(~ sex) +   labs(     title = "HIV cases by age and sex, 2022",     x = "Age group",     y = "Cases"   )

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: can also query multiple years compare trends time. example, compare national totals year.
code: syphilis_years <- get_atlas(   disease   = "adult syphilis",   geography = "state",   year      = 2018:2022 )  head(syphilis_years) #>                        indicator year geography    data_status #> 1 Primary and Secondary Syphilis 2018   Alabama Not Suppressed #> 2 Primary and Secondary Syphilis 2019   Alabama Not Suppressed #> 3 Primary and Secondary Syphilis 2020   Alabama Not Suppressed #> 4 Primary and Secondary Syphilis 2021   Alabama Not Suppressed #> 5 Primary and Secondary Syphilis 2022   Alabama Not Suppressed #> 6 Primary and Secondary Syphilis 2018    Alaska Not Suppressed #>          race_ethnicity        sex            age                transmission #> 1 All races/ethnicities Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories #> 2 All races/ethnicities Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories #> 3 All races/ethnicities Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories #> 4 All races/ethnicities Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories #> 5 All races/ethnicities Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories #> 6 All races/ethnicities Both sexes All age groups All transmission categories #>   rate100000 cases population lowerci_rate upperci_rate rse lowerci_cases #> 1        9.8   477    4887681           NA           NA  NA            NA #> 2       12.6   618    4903185           NA           NA  NA            NA #> 3       10.5   529    5024279           NA           NA  NA            NA #> 4       15.1   761    5039877           NA           NA  NA            NA #> 5       23.5  1190    5074296           NA           NA  NA            NA #> 6        7.5    55     735139           NA           NA  NA            NA #>   upperci_cases fips #> 1            NA   01 #> 2            NA   01 #> 3            NA   01 #> 4            NA   01 #> 5            NA   01 #> 6            NA   02 syphilis_trend <- syphilis_years %>%   group_by(year, indicator) %>%   summarize(     cases = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  syphilis_trend #> # A tibble: 15 × 3 #>     year indicator                                 cases #>    <dbl> <fct>                                     <dbl> #>  1  2018 Primary and Secondary Syphilis            35447 #>  2  2018 Early Non-Primary, Non-Secondary Syphilis 39119 #>  3  2018 Unknown Duration or Late Syphilis         40285 #>  4  2019 Primary and Secondary Syphilis            39327 #>  5  2019 Early Non-Primary, Non-Secondary Syphilis 42118 #>  6  2019 Unknown Duration or Late Syphilis         47473 #>  7  2020 Primary and Secondary Syphilis            41942 #>  8  2020 Early Non-Primary, Non-Secondary Syphilis 43486 #>  9  2020 Unknown Duration or Late Syphilis         47256 #> 10  2021 Primary and Secondary Syphilis            54108 #> 11  2021 Early Non-Primary, Non-Secondary Syphilis 52247 #> 12  2021 Unknown Duration or Late Syphilis         68691 #> 13  2022 Primary and Secondary Syphilis            59404 #> 14  2022 Early Non-Primary, Non-Secondary Syphilis 57450 #> 15  2022 Unknown Duration or Late Syphilis         88121 syphilis_trend%>% ggplot(mapping= aes(x = year, y = cases, color=indicator, group=indicator)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   labs(     title = "Adult syphilis cases over time",     x = "Year",     y = "Cases"   )

what: Working with censored or missing values
title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: CDC AtlasPlus may suppress censor values, especially small counts. Depending disease geography, cases rate100000 may missing. common first step inspect missingness.
code: chlamydia_county %>%   summarize(     n_rows = n(),     missing_cases = sum(is.na(cases)),     missing_rates = sum(is.na(rate100000))   ) #>   n_rows missing_cases missing_rates #> 1   3231            19            19

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: available diseases stratification variables depend CDC AtlasPlus. Common disease values include:
code: get_atlas(disease = "hiv", geography = "state", year = 2022) get_atlas(disease = "chlamydia", geography = "state", year = 2022) get_atlas(disease = "gonorrhea", geography = "state", year = 2022) get_atlas(disease = "adult syphilis", geography = "state", year = 2022) get_atlas(disease = "tuberculosis", geography = "state", year = 2022)

title: Getting started with CDCAtlas
text: getting comfortable basic queries, consider: querying multiple diseases comparing state county results using sex, age, race/ethnicity stratification joining AtlasPlus data Census spatial data using advanced vignettes tract-level extrapolation geographic access analyses See ?get_atlas full argument documentation.

text: Hemmige V (2026). CDCAtlas: package downloading CDC Atlas data R. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/VagishHemmige/CDCAtlas.
code: @Manual{,   title = {CDCAtlas: A package for downloading CDC Atlas data into R},   author = {Vagish Hemmige},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/VagishHemmige/CDCAtlas}, }

title: CDCAtlas
text: CDCAtlas R package retrieving selected public health surveillance data CDC’s AtlasPlus platform.

what: What is the CDC Atlas database?
title: CDCAtlas
text: AtlasPlus interactive CDC data tool provides access reported data HIV, sexually transmitted infections, tuberculosis, viral hepatitis, selected social determinants health. allows users explore data maps, charts, tables, downloadable reports. package provides programmatic interface selected AtlasPlus data researchers can query, cache, analyze results directly R rather working web interface, requires downloading individual files require manual cleanup importing R.

title: CDCAtlas
text: CDCAtlas provides polite, cache-aware R client selected CDC AtlasPlus endpoints. AtlasPlus stable, public API. package aims safe respectful usage: identifies via custom User-Agent applies rate limiting retry backoff caches responses reduce server traffic handles schema changes gracefully possible

title: CDCAtlas
text: can install development version CDCAtlas GitHub :
code: install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("VagishHemmige/CDCAtlas")

title: CDCAtlas
text: basic example shows solve common problem:
code: library(CDCAtlas)  gonorrhea_us <- get_atlas(   disease = "gonorrhea",   geography = "state",   year = 2022 )

what: Retrieve STI surveillance data from CDC AtlasPlus — get_atlas
title: Retrieve STI surveillance data from CDC AtlasPlus — get_atlas
text: get_atlas() provides programmatic interface CDC AtlasPlus platform, returning case counts, population denominators, rate estimates sexually transmitted infection (STI) surveillance. function submits payload AtlasPlus data endpoint parses response tidy tibble.

title: Retrieve STI surveillance data from CDC AtlasPlus — get_atlas
code: get_atlas(   disease,   geography,   year,   stratify_by = NULL,   extrapolate_to_tract = FALSE )

title: Retrieve STI surveillance data from CDC AtlasPlus — get_atlas
text: disease single disease name (e.g. "gonorrhea"). Multiple diseases currently supported single request. geography Character scalar. One "national", "region", "state", "county", "msa". year Integer vector years. Valid values 2000 2023. Can single year (e.g., 2022) sequence (e.g., 2018:2022). stratify_by Optional character vector. Zero, one, two "age", "race", "sex". NULL, totals (unstratified) returned. extrapolate_to_tract Logical scalar. FALSE, return AtlasPlus results requested geography. TRUE, pass returned data package's tract extrapolation workflow. Extrapolation requires geography set county.

title: Retrieve STI surveillance data from CDC AtlasPlus — get_atlas
text: tibble following columns: indicator Disease/condition name (factor) year Calendar year (numeric) geography Geographic unit name (factor) data_status Data quality flags (factor) race_ethnicity Race/ethnicity category (factor, stratified) sex Sex category (factor, stratified) age Age group (factor, stratified) transmission Transmission category (factor) rate100000 Incidence rate per 100,000 (numeric) cases Case count (numeric) population Population denominator (numeric) lowerci_rate, upperci_rate 95\% CI bounds rate (numeric) rse Relative standard error (numeric) lowerci_cases, upperci_cases 95\% CI bounds cases (numeric) FIPS code (character)  Columns naming conventions may differ depending CDC schema changes. function attempts standardize guarantee historical compatibility years.

title: Retrieve STI surveillance data from CDC AtlasPlus — get_atlas
text: function supports multiple diseases, geographic levels, year ranges, optional stratification. Stratified queries expand results requested combinations (e.g., age race). possible, numeric fields returned double identifiers character. default, function returns data directly requested AtlasPlus geography. extrapolate_to_tract = TRUE, county-level results post-processed census tract-level estimates using helper functions package. tract estimates derived estimates directly returned CDC AtlasPlus. Supported diseases "chlamydia" "gonorrhea" "adult syphilis" "congenital syphilis" "tuberculosis" "hiv" (provide confirmed diagnoses) "estimate" (estimated HIV counts) "hepatitis" Geography "national" "region" "state" "county" "msa" (metropolitan statistical area) Stratification CDC AtlasPlus API supports stratification one two dimensions: "age" "race" "sex" Stratification increases number returned rows. example, stratify_by = c("race", "sex") returns race × sex combinations geography-year pair. function reverse-engineered wrapper around AtlasPlus public query system. CDC publish official API specification, internal identifiers may change without notice. CDC modifies backend schema, requests may fail. Please report issues via GitHub.

title: Retrieve STI surveillance data from CDC AtlasPlus — get_atlas
code: if (FALSE) { # National gonorrhea rates for 2018–2022 get_atlas(disease = "gonorrhea", geography = "national", year = 2018:2022)  # State-level chlamydia, stratified by race get_atlas(   disease = "chlamydia",   geography = "state",   year = 2022,   stratify_by = "race" )  get_atlas(   disease = "gonorrhea",   geography = "county",   year = 2022,   extrapolate_to_tract = TRUE ) }
